table_metadata.table_description: this is a table going back to 2021 containing all accounts and activities each month and the spend tied to them.
table_metadata.special_rules: IMPORTANT: There is no 'total_monthly_spend' column. To find total monthly marketing spend, you MUST aggregate and calculate the SUM of the 'Amount' column, grouped by 'Year' and 'Month'.

schema_definition.Column Description: primary key index - likely will not need to be referenced
a combined string containing the company, department, account, and activity codes together. Could be useful for referencing
the company unit the spend was directed to
the department of the company the spend was dircted to
the account of the company the spend is attributed to.
the specific activity of the account the spend was for.
An indicator to determine if the spend was 'working dollars' or 'non-working dollars'.
The name of the marketing tactic tied to that specific account/activity code combo
schema_definition.Range of Outcomes: A specifc Tactic name, like Broadcast or Online. see below in the dictionary for all the tactics

schema_definition.Column Description: The name of the marketing subtactic tied to this specific account/activity code combo
schema_definition.Range of Outcomes: A specifc Subtactic, for Online it might be Search. see below in the doctionary for all the subtactics.

schema_definition.Column Description: An indicator of what purpose the spend is torwards.
schema_definition.Range of Outcomes: Direct Response (Trying to get the customer to Subscriber), Brand (raising general brand awareness, not tied to closing), Display (similar to brand spend, displaying brand images), Social (Specific to social media spend), Production (related to producing commercials, etc.), Sales Support, Other, Contractual

schema_definition.Column Description: the month the spend took place in
year the spend took place in.

The transactional amount spent. To calculate total spend, total monthly spend, or total marketing spend, you must use SUM(amount). Positive indicates we spent money, negative indicates we received money back.
